subject: Re: Trying to create stacks for IMOD-invert tilt angles issue
body:
Ah, perhaps I'm misunderstanding SerialEM's default behavior. Does it not 
sort - to +? I haven't seen + to - ordering so far.

If the error is not the result of incorrect parsing, then I'm afraid there 
is no easy way to fix it in this version. To match the different tilt order 
in your alignments, you'd have to either invert the tilt angles in the MDOC 
so Warp sorts them in reverse order, or invert the line order of the 
wrpMovieName column in the generated tomoSTAR files after import. Both are 
doable with some scripting, but really annoying without.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Friday, July 24, 2020 at 12:32:53 AM UTC+2, Jessica Rabuck-Gibbons wrote:
>
> Hi Dmitry,
>
> I can share my tilt series and the .mdoc files with you—what would be the 
> easiest way to do this?
>
> The .rawtlt/.st from WARP is going in order from - to + (so correct, but 
> my original tilt series (and the order in the mdoc) goes from + to -. 
>
> Thanks,
> Jessica
>
>
> On Thursday, July 23, 2020 at 3:23:44 PM UTC-7 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Jessica,
>>
>> Warp should sort the tilts from negative to positive based on the angles 
>> appearing in the MDOC file. If this doesn't happen, I assume the file is 
>> not being parsed correctly. Can you share one of them (anonymized if 
>> necessary)? The GUI is misleading regarding how the other options affect 
>> the "create stack" functionality – neither "don't invert angles", nor the 
>> pixel size or dose have any effect.
>>
>> Is it possible that SerialEM has some additional setting for stacking the 
>> tilts in reversed order?
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>>
>> On Friday, July 24, 2020 at 12:16:18 AM UTC+2, Jessica Rabuck-Gibbons 
>> wrote:
>>>
>>> Hello,
>>>
>>> Whenever I try to export my tilt series to IMOD after doing motion 
>>> correction and CTF estimation in WARP, the .rawtlt and the .st files are in 
>>> the opposite order of my raw tilt series that is directly from Serial EM. 
>>> I've tried combinations of checking the "don't invert tilt angle" button 
>>> along with checking the handedness of the tilt series, but nothing seems to 
>>> export a .st file or a .rawtlt file that matches the order of my original, 
>>> raw tilt series. It almost seems to be ignoring the order in my .mdoc file. 
>>> Will WARP recognize that its output is "backwards" from my original data, 
>>> (so I could safely apply IMOD alignments that I have done on the original 
>>> tilt series to the WARP'd data, and reconstruct my tilt series in WARP)? 
>>> We're running version 1.0.9. Thank you for your help!
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Jess
>>>
>>> Jessica Rabuck-Gibbons, PhD
>>> Postdoctoral Associate | Williamson Lab | The Scripps Research Institute
>>>
>>
